mechanism_narrative: Declining nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate wage income and foreshadow weaker corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of months when PAYEMS declines from the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% signals excess labor market slack that triggers negative earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in consumer prices and industrial production generate monetary policy restraint expectations that compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction of CPIAUCSL and INDPRO achieves a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in real retail sales reflect consumer demand weakness that amplifies equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, positive 5-day forward returns follow month-over-month RSAFS declines in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal durable goods demand and construction sector confidence that translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of months when HOUST increases from the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO falls more than 1% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling industrial production defines a contractionary non-farm sector dynamic that forces equity risk repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of UNRATE increase and INDPRO decrease produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer prices reduce real corporate earnings expectations and heighten the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns follow month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases in more than 50% of observations.
